description: Fires when a visitor submits a form on your site. By default the tag fires for any form on any page; you can add conditions to fire only for a specific form (Form ID, Form URL, Form Classes, Form Name), specific page (Page URL, Page path, Page hostname), or other criteria (Referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor). See Piwik PRO help: Form submission trigger.

condition_types.description: Fires when a visitor submits a form with the selected ID
Fires when a visitor submits a form that sends a request to the selected page URL
Fires when a visitor submits a form with the selected CSS class
Fires when a visitor submits a form with the selected name
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page with the selected page URL
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page with the selected page path
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page hosted on the selected domain
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page to which they were directed by the selected internal referrer
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page to which they were directed by the selected external referrer
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page to which they were directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign)
Fires when a visitor submits a form on a page to which they were directed by the selected traffic source. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct
Fires when a visitor is a returning visitor and submits a form (stg_returning_visitor cookie)
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Track form submissions (contact, signup, newsletter, checkout)
Fire conversion or analytics tags when a specific form is submitted
Limit tag firing to forms on certain pages or with certain IDs/names
common_use_cases: Lead generation and lead form tracking
Checkout or multi-step form step tracking
Form submission analytics by form ID or page path
best_practices: Use Form ID, Form Name, or Form URL conditions to target a specific form and avoid firing on every form
Add Page path or Page URL conditions when the same form appears on multiple pages and you only want some
Use descriptive trigger names that indicate the form or purpose (e.g. Contact Form Submit, Newsletter Signup)

create_trigger.description: Create a form submission trigger. By default fires on any form submit; add conditions to restrict by form (Form ID/URL/Classes/Name) or page (Page URL/path, Referrer, Campaign, etc.).

trigger_type.description: Must be 'form_submission' for form submission triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Optional. Use Form ID, Form URL, Form Classes, Form Name for the form; Page URL, Page path, Referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor for context.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing form submission trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Optional; when empty, fires on every form submission. Use Form ID, Form URL, Form Classes, Form Name to target a specific form; Page URL, Page path, Page hostname, Referrer, External referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor, or variable-based for context.

structure.variable_id: UUID of the variable (e.g. form ID, form URL, page URL variable)

conditions.recommendation: Include at least one form-related condition (Form ID, Form Name, Form URL, or Form Classes) to avoid firing on all forms site-wide.

form_submission_any.description: Fire on any form submission (no conditions)

form_submission_contact_form.description: Fire only when form ID contains contact-form

form_submission_with_page_path.description: Fire when form is submitted on a page whose path contains /signup/

common_mistakes: Firing on every form (no Form ID/Name/URL/Classes condition) when only one form should be tracked
Using Form URL to mean the page URL (Form URL is the form action / request target)
Expecting the trigger to fire on form-like elements that are not actual HTML form submissions

possible_causes: Conditions too restrictive (e.g. Form ID mismatch)
Form submitted via AJAX without firing submit event
Form has no id/name/action that matches your conditions
debugging_steps: Test with empty conditions to confirm form submissions are detected
Verify form id, name, or action URL in the page source
Ensure the form triggers a native submit event if your setup relies on it
